text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris The Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity May 31 2026 This weekend May 30th 31st the Church celebrates the Solemnity of the Most Holy Trinity Following the Solemnity of Pentecost and the descent of the Holy Spirit we now dwell in the midst of this great mystery of the Trinity One God Three Divine Persons We rejoice together in the unity of the Father who has adopted us in the Son whose brethren and joint heirs we are and in the Holy Spirit who dwells within us and governs our lives Next Saturday June 6th five men will be ordained by Bishop Rhoades as priests for our diocese All are welcome to join us in the Mass of Ordination this Saturday at 11 AM at the Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception in Fort Wayne Please continue praying for more priestly and religious vocations from the young men of our diocese Never underestimate the power of our prayers and penances in to support those willing to say Yes to the Lord's call This coming Thursday marks my own ten year anniversary of ordination I look forward to joining with my two classmates at Saturday's ordination as we welcome these five men into this beautiful brotherhood that is the priesthood Corpus Christi Procession On Sunday June 7th the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Corpus Christi Following our 10 AM Mass on Sunday June 7th we will hold our annual Corpus Christi Procession In every Eucharistic Procession we follow after the Lord physically as we pledge to do so spiritually We also give public witness to our acceptance of the Lord's words The bread that I will give is my flesh for the life of the world John 651 National Consecration to the Sacred Heart On Thursday June 11th the Catholic bishops of the United States will consecrate our nation to the Sacred Heart of Jesus during their annual meeting in honor of the 250th anniversary year of our nation's founding In making this act of consecration the bishops are entrusting our country anew to the love mercy and care of Christ They are asking that His Sacred Heart heal division strengthen faith and renew our nation in truth and charity Devotion to the Sacred Heart reminds us that the Faith is about the love of Jesus Christ poured out for the world upon the Cross His love is made ever present to us today in the Holy Eucharist The Sacred Heart calls us to a life of deeper prayer reparation for sin and love of our neighbor This consecration is an invitation for every Catholic family parish and individual to entrust to our Lord the hope healing and renewal of both our Church and our nation Graduation Mass On Sunday June 14th at the 10 AM Mass we will celebrate our annual Graduate's Mass for our graduating High School Seniors If you have a high school senior who is graduating and would like to be honored at this Mass please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information Open Positions at the Parish With our parish renovations we have posted a second custodial position to assist with the upcoming increased cleaning responsibilities This position focuses on maintaining the Schaefer Parish Center including our Religious Education Classrooms and Gymnasium Starting at 14 per hour with an increase to 15 after four months this position offers flexible working hours Please contact the Parish Office (260)824-1380 for more information We've also posted a full time position for a Pastoral Associate of Youth and Parish Life Following our parish synod's overwhelming feedback for increased focus on youth retention this position is turning this feedback into a priority We as a parish family are making an investment in our future with this full time position For the full job posting please visit our parish website or the diocesan careers page Please keep this job search in your prayers Saturday May 30
